seoTitle: How to Change Account Type in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Update an account's type in QuickBooks Online to keep your chart of accounts accurate for tax reporting and financial categorization.
intro: Getting account types right in QuickBooks Online matters for accurate tax reporting and clear financial categorization, especially as a business grows or its needs change. This guide covers how to locate an account and change its type without disrupting existing records.

steps.heading: Open your chart of accounts
steps.body: Navigate to the account settings area in QuickBooks Online where all your accounts are listed.

steps.heading: Select the account you want to edit
steps.body: Find the specific account whose type needs updating and open its edit options.

steps.heading: Change the account type
steps.body: Choose the correct account type and detail type from the available options to better reflect the account's financial purpose.

steps.heading: Double-check the categorization
steps.body: Review the new classification to make sure income and expenses will be tracked correctly going forward.

steps.heading: Save your changes
steps.body: Confirm the update so the account reflects its new type across reports and financial statements.

faq.question: Will changing an account type affect past transactions?
faq.answer: Changing the type reclassifies the account itself, which can affect how past transactions appear in reports, so it's worth reviewing your financial statements after the change.

faq.question: Can I change any account type in QuickBooks Online?
faq.answer: Most account types can be edited, though some restrictions may apply depending on the account's history and whether it's linked to bank feeds.

faq.question: Why would I need to change an account type?
faq.answer: Common reasons include correcting a setup mistake, adapting to a business expansion, or adjusting categorization for tax purposes.

faq.question: What happens if I choose the wrong account type by mistake?
faq.answer: You can typically go back into the account settings and correct the type again, though it's best to review reports afterward to confirm accuracy.
